hawkish_phrases: inflation had increased to 3.0 per cent in August
risks to the inflation outlook...were widely regarded as being tilted to the upside
a symmetric application of the PEPP framework would call for a more substantial reduction in the pace of purchases
if supply bottlenecks lasted longer and fed through into higher than anticipated wage rises, price pressures could be more persistent
even without the PEPP, the overall monetary policy stance remained highly accommodative
dovish_phrases: this notable upswing in inflation was assessed as largely temporary
inflation was still foreseen to remain far below the two per cent target over the projection horizon
underlying price pressures were building up only slowly
an accommodative monetary policy stance remained necessary to offset the negative impact of the pandemic on inflation
there was a risk that a reduction in PEPP purchases...would be interpreted as tapering, which could result in a tightening of financing conditions
summary: The Governing Council decided to moderately reduce the pace of PEPP net asset purchases from the previous two quarters while maintaining all other policy measures, judging that favourable financing conditions could be preserved despite inflation reaching 3.0%, which was assessed as largely temporary with medium-term inflation still well below the 2% target.
